name: The Redline
descriptionSeed: The violent frontier of the Marches — but honed, not feral. A warrior culture whose entire society is disciplined conflict: duels settle law, standing is won on the training-ground, and the Edge is taught to children as literacy. What rides with the violence here is BODY — the perfected physical, movement as language. Terrifying to a stranger, and rigorously fair once you learn the forms. Not the Gralloch's lawlessness; the opposite — violence with rules so strict they're almost peace.
encounterFlavor: Honed conflict as culture — a formal duel with witnesses, a child besting an adult by the forms, a challenge you can accept or honorably decline, a warrior reading your stance before your words.

encounterSeeds.hint: A challenge issued here becomes public record the moment it is spoken

questSeeds: You're challenged on arrival by custom — how you handle a duel you didn't want (fight, decline, turn it to a Contest) sets your whole standing in the Marches
A Marcher champion is being pushed toward a war they know is unjust; they want an outsider to give them a way to refuse without dishonor
The Edge is being taught to someone who shouldn't have it — a person whose rage the discipline can't hold; the Marchers are afraid of what they made
